action.reasoning: Observation: We are at iteration 26 with only ~7.5 minutes remaining and 4 iterations left. The best-ever eval is rmse_z500=1018.63 from iteration 5 (checkpoint lost). Our strongest available checkpoint is best_retrained_bias.pt scoring 1026.14. Analysis: All recent single-model and ensemble attempts without TTA score 1026-1060, still above 1018. The only untapped lever is loss-function alignment: the evaluation metric uses latitude-weighted RMSE, but every model was trained with unweighted MSE. Fine-tuning the best checkpoint for just one epoch with latitude-weighted MSE can align the model with the metric and potentially shave off the remaining ~7-8 points. With batch_size=256, one epoch should take ~100 seconds, keeping total iteration time under 4 minutes and leaving a small buffer. Decision: Write a lightweight finetune.py that loads best_retrained.pt, runs one epoch of weighted MSE with low LR (1e-4), saves to finetuned.pt, dry-runs predict.py, and immediately submits for evaluation.
action.command: python finetune.py && python predict.py --data_path temp_test --checkpoint checkpoints_retrain/finetuned.pt --output temp_test/out_finetuned.json
